title: Rate limiting, error shapes, pagination edge cases
description: Harden the API surface for production use. Implement rate limiting headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) even if not enforcing limits (clients expect these headers). Verify all error responses are {"error": "message"} JSON with correct Content-Type. Test pagination edge cases: empty results, single item, exact page boundary.
design: Error handler: AppError enum that maps to HTTP status + JSON body. Rate limit headers: emit with reasonable values even without enforcement (1000 req/5min per client). Pagination: validate max_id/since_id as valid integers, return 422 if malformed (not 500).
acceptance_criteria: All error 4xx responses have Content-Type: application/json and {"error": "..."} body; X-RateLimit-* headers present on all API responses; pagination with limit=1 works correctly; fetching past the end of a timeline returns empty array not 404; malformed IDs in URL params return 404 not 500
close_reason: Query param deserialization fixed, pagination working
